commit.message:
Add repeated sentence filter to ML inference pipeline (#675)

* Add repeated sentence filter to ML inference pipeline

Detect and handle LLM outputs that have gone into a loop, repeating the
same sentence or alternating between two sentences. The filter:

- Caps any single sentence at max_repeats (default 3) occurrences
- Detects and collapses A-B-A-B alternating patterns
- Returns None for severely repetitive text (<30% unique content)
- Integrates into bad_result() for early rejection (>50% repetition)
- Integrates into _checked_infer() cleaning chain as outermost filter

https://claude.ai/code/session_013ygPnHVfMpRWsWV4FWAC1X

* Fix repeated sentence filter: alternating detection, None handling, simplify cleaning

Three fixes to the repeated sentence filter:

1. has_severe_repetition now detects alternating A-B-A-B loops, not just
   single-sentence dominance. Checks for exactly 2 distinct sentences that
   together exceed the threshold and actually alternate (adjacent differ).

2. _checked_infer returns [] instead of [(infer_type, None)] when
   remove_repeated_sentences returns None, so the downstream consumer in
   generate_appeal.py correctly skips the result rather than propagating
   None text through template generation.

3. Simplified remove_repeated_sentences to just per-sentence capping,
   removing the fragile A-B-specific collapse block. This naturally handles
   A-B-A-B, A-B-C-A-B-D, and any other repetitive pattern by limiting each
   unique sentence to max_repeats occurrences.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013ygPnHVfMpRWsWV4FWAC1X

---------

commit.message:
fix: remove baked-in rounded corners from icon, use full-bleed background

macOS applies its own squircle mask to dock icons. Having rounded corners
baked into the icon created an ugly double-border effect. Now the dark
background fills edge-to-edge so macOS clips it cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix walk() double-counting in getCurrentPeriodData

Apply same "Summary is authoritative, skip children" rule to walk()
in extractTotals(). Previously, walk() recursed into Section children
AND captured the Section Summary, causing double-counting for revenue
streams like Civille (~$70,619 shown vs ~$44,210 actual).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(generator): assign explicit priority to all 16 components + enforce with tests (#21)

componentPriority previously only listed 7 components. The 9 new advanced
components all fell into the 'unknown = sort alphabetically' bucket, which
could produce non-deterministic registration order if new components were
added in the future, and silently placed auth/tenancy middleware components
AFTER the HTTP server (wrong — middleware must be registered before HTTP wires
routes).

Correct ordering rationale:
  0. foundry-postgres — sets the DB; everything else needs it
  1. foundry-auth-jwt — JWT validation middleware
  2. foundry-auth-spicedb — SpiceDB authz middleware
  3. foundry-tenancy — row-isolation middleware wraps all handlers
  4. foundry-http — HTTP server wires in registered middleware
  5. foundry-grpc — gRPC server (parallel to HTTP)
  6. foundry-health — observability
  7. foundry-metrics — observability
  8. foundry-events — event bus client
  9. foundry-kafka — Kafka producer/consumer
 10. foundry-nats — NATS pub/sub
 11. foundry-redis — Redis client
 12. foundry-redis-streams — Redis Streams consumer
 13. foundry-temporal — Temporal.io workflow client
 14. foundry-graph-age — Apache AGE graph (uses PG extension)
 15. foundry-service-router — routes traffic to registered services (last)

Tests added:
  - TestSortComponents_AllComponentsHavePriority — guard against future
    components being added without an explicit priority assignment
  - TestSortComponents_AuthBeforeHTTP — auth/tenancy before HTTP
  - TestSortComponents_ServiceRouterLast — service-router is last

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bullets dobles — strip todo carácter no-alfanumérico al inicio

Cambiar regex de cleanBullets y stripBullet a /^[^a-zA-Z0-9áéíóúñ...]+/
que elimina CUALQUIER carácter no-letra/número del inicio del string.
Funciona sin importar qué bullet Unicode devuelva la IA.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-retry Hello handshake for stuck-connecting peers (#176)

* feat: auto-retry Hello handshake for peers stuck without slot assignment

Add a third watchdog tier to detect peers that are active (audio flowing)
but whose Hello handshake has never completed (identity unknown). At 5s,
re-send Hello; at 15s, force a reconnect. This allows the system to self-repair
when ICE succeeds but the sync DataChannel handshake fails due to TURN timeouts,
eliminating the need for manual reconnect to recover slot assignments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Hello soft retries on every watchdog tick

Add hello_retry_sent flag to PeerState so the soft Hello retry fires
only once per connection attempt instead of on every 5s watchdog tick.

commit.message:
feat: integrate Sentry crash & error reporting (#193)

* feat: integrate Sentry crash & error reporting

Add @sentry/electron for unified crash reporting across main and renderer
processes. Captures JS exceptions, native addon segfaults (minidumps),
and queues events offline for later delivery.

- Main process: DSN-guarded init with release tagging and environment detection
- Renderer: Session Replay (error-only), browser tracing, React integration
- electron-log → Sentry breadcrumb bridge for full crash context
- ErrorBoundary reports uncaught React errors to Sentry
- @sentry/vite-plugin uploads source maps during CI release builds
- Global vitest setup auto-mocks Sentry modules for test isolation

Closes #186

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: apply oxlint fix and oxfmt formatting

- Fix array-type lint error: Plugin[] → Array<Plugin>
- Apply oxfmt formatting to new test files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: upgrade CI Node.js from 22 to 24

@sentry/electron transitively pulls in @types/node@25 via OpenTelemetry
instrumentation packages. On CI with Node 22, tsgo resolves these types
instead of the built-in Node 22 types, causing ChildProcess and Dirent
type mismatches. Upgrading CI to Node 24 (matching local dev) aligns
the runtime with the resolved @types/node version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pin @types/node to v22 via pnpm override, revert CI to Node 22

@sentry/electron transitively pulls in @types/node@25 via OpenTelemetry
instrumentation packages. tsgo resolves this hoisted version, which has
incompatible ChildProcess and Dirent types vs the Node 22 built-ins used
in CI. Pin @types/node to ^22 via pnpm.overrides so the transitive dep
can't pollute type resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: upgrade CI Node.js to 24, add @types/node audit rule

Upgrade CI and release workflows from Node 22 to Node 24 to match local
dev. The @types/node ^22 pnpm override ensures type resolution stays
consistent regardless of Node runtime version.

Add dependency rule: check for transitive @types/node pollution after
adding new packages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: align @types/node override to ^24 to match CI Node version

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: skip Chromatic when no UI files changed

Add a 'ui' path filter that matches packages/ui/** and
apps/desktop/src/renderer/components/**. Chromatic only runs
when these paths are touched, saving CI time on backend-only
or config-only changes.

typed messages that route to the appropriate RoomManager handlers.

commit.message:
Add OAuth2 Authorization Code flow for patron onboarding (#9) (#9)

Patrons can now authorize directly in their browser instead of
manually running curl commands. The server handles the token exchange
and session activation automatically via begin_oauth / check_oauth_status
tools and an /oauth/callback HTTP endpoint.

- New oauth_flow.py: HMAC-signed state tokens, Schwab token exchange,
  account hash lookup, in-memory pending state with 600s TTL
- Two new free tools: begin_oauth, check_oauth_status (14 tools total)
- /oauth/callback custom route handles Schwab redirect
- HMAC signing key derived from sha256(operator_nsec)
- 18 new tests (68 total), ruff clean
- Version bump to 0.7.0

commit.message:
fix: Ensure channel lead respawns when user posts in topic channel [Midtown !2171] (#1902)

* fix: Ensure channel lead respawns when user posts in topic channel

Three-layer fix for channel leads not auto-spawning when users post in
topic channels:

1. Exclude channel lead sessions from GC — they are long-lived and
   should always be available for resume, regardless of resume_on_startup
   flag (health.rs)

2. Death handler preserves channel lead state — don't set
   resume_on_startup=false for channel leads, and clear the stale
   session ID in channel_lead_sessions instead of removing the key
   entirely (which would make ensure_channel_leads_alive forget the
   channel needs a lead) (mod.rs)

3. Add expedite mechanism for topic channel leads — when a user posts
   in a topic channel and the channel lead is dead, clear its respawn
   cooldown so ensure_channel_leads_alive fires on the next tick. This
   mirrors the main lead's expedite_lead_respawn_on_user_message pattern
   (rpc_channel.rs)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address review feedback for channel lead respawn

- effects.rs: Preserve channel_lead_sessions key on dead session cleanup
  and spawn failure (insert empty string instead of removing)
- rpc_channel.rs: Fix case mismatch in stop_times.remove — use
  to_lowercase() to match insertion pattern
- health.rs: Update check_for_state_gc doc comment to document
  channel-lead session exemption from GC
- docs/architecture.md: Document channel-lead GC exemption in daemon
  state garbage collection section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix events missing from site due to category filter mismatch

Admin shows all docs in the events collection (no category filter).
Site query also filtered by category == 'events', which silently dropped
any document missing that field (e.g. older entries, pre-field additions).

The events collection only holds events, so the category filter is
redundant. Dropping it makes all active events visible on the site
regardless of whether the category field is set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Upgrade MCP transport from SSE to Streamable HTTP

SSE was deprecated by MCP (May 2025). Streamable HTTP provides:
- Single /mcp endpoint (replaces /sse + /sse/messages)
- No persistent connections — better scalability
- Per-request validation — better security
- HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 support

Changes:
- mcp_server.py: transport="sse" → transport="streamable-http"
- requirements.txt: mcp[cli]>=1.6.0 → mcp[cli]>=1.8.0
- All docs: --transport sse → --transport http, /sse → /mcp
- CHANGELOG: add v0.2.0

commit.message:
feat(mcp): transport abstraction, stdio/UDS transports, and OAuth fixes (#721)

* feat(mcp): transport abstraction, stdio/UDS transports, and OAuth fixes

Extract McpTransport trait from HTTP-coupled McpClient, enabling pluggable
transport backends. Implements stdio and Unix domain socket transports for
local MCP server integration, fixes OAuth discovery per RFC 9728, and adds
SSRF protection.

Transport abstraction (Step 2):
- McpTransport trait with send(), shutdown(), supports_http_features()
- HttpMcpTransport extracted from McpClient with SSE parsing, session tracking
- Shared JSON-RPC framing helpers (write_jsonrpc_line, spawn_jsonrpc_reader)
- McpClient refactored to hold Arc<dyn McpTransport>

Stdio transport (#652, Step 4):
- StdioMcpTransport spawns child process, communicates via stdin/stdout
- McpProcessManager for lifecycle management with exponential backoff restart
- Background stderr drain task for debug logging

Unix domain socket transport (#134, Step 5):
- UnixMcpTransport connects to existing Unix sockets
- Reuses shared JSON-RPC framing from transport.rs

HTML error body sanitization (#263, Step 1):
- sanitize_error_body() detects HTML, strips control chars, truncates to 500

Custom headers (#639, Step 3):
- headers field on McpServerConfig, merged into every HTTP request
- --header CLI arg for `mcp add`

Config and CLI updates (Step 6):
- McpTransportConfig tagged enum (Http/Stdio/Unix) with serde support
- EffectiveTransport for zero-copy config dispatch
- CLI: --transport, --command, --arg, --env, --socket flags for `mcp add`
- `mcp list` shows transport type

OAuth fixes (#299, Step 8):
- Multi-strategy discovery (401-based, RFC 9728, direct)
- RFC 8707 resource parameter in auth and refresh flows
- SSRF protection with IPv4-mapped IPv6 bypass detection
- Well-known URI construction per RFC 8414

Closes #652, #134, #639, #263, #299

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mcp): address audit findings from crate review

- Fix SSRF bypass: make validate_url_safe async with DNS resolution to
  block hostnames that resolve to private/link-local IPs
- Fix UTF-8 truncation: use char-based truncation in sanitize_error_body
  to avoid panicking on multi-byte characters
- Fix SSE parser: process only complete lines to handle chunks split
  across boundaries, add 10MB buffer size limit
- Add debug_assert for transport type mismatch in new_with_config
- Propagate custom headers in new_with_transport constructor
- Deduplicate effective_transport() calls in CLI list command
- Gate test-only accessors with #[cfg(test)] to eliminate dead_code warnings
- Document JSON-RPC notification id:0 limitation in protocol.rs
- Document total backoff wait time (31s) in process.rs
- Add regression test for multi-byte UTF-8 truncation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mcp): address PR review findings from Copilot, Gemini, and zmanian

Moderate/High fixes:
- Plumb custom headers through new_authenticated constructor
- Restrict HTTP to localhost only in validate_url_safe (prevent
  plaintext credential leaks over non-localhost HTTP)
- Add mcp_process_manager.shutdown_all() to app shutdown path to
  prevent orphaning stdio child processes
- Validate discovered authorization_url before opening browser
  (prevent malicious MCP server redirecting to phishing page)

Medium fixes:
- Upgrade debug_assert to assert in new_with_config (fires in release)
- Remove pending map entry on Ok(Err(_)) in stdio/unix send() to avoid
  stale entries and unnecessary 30s waits
- Shut down old transport in try_restart() before spawning replacement
- Redact env var values in mcp list --verbose (may contain secrets)
- Drain pending requests on shutdown to wake waiters immediately
- Add IPv6 link-local, site-local, unique-local, and documentation
  ranges to is_dangerous_ip SSRF protection

Low fixes:
- Truncate logged JSON parse error lines to 200 chars (prevent
  sensitive data in logs)
- Remove misleading shutdown comment in unix_transport
- Use tempfile::tempdir() instead of hardcoded /tmp/ path in test
- Adopt main's improved sanitize_error_body (HTML tag stripping,
  200-char truncation with char_indices)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mcp): gate unix_transport with #[cfg(unix)] for Windows compat

- Add #[cfg(unix)] to unix_transport module declaration
- Add #[cfg(unix)]/#[cfg(not(unix))] branches in app.rs for Unix
  socket MCP server setup
- Remove unused sanitize_error_body import in client.rs tests

[skip-regression-check]

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
